core_mechanism: Gamma-convergence (asymptotic variational limit) of a singularly-perturbed energy functional in which a diverging double-well potential term (scaled like 1/epsilon) competes with a higher-order gradient/curvature penalty (scaled like epsilon), so that as the small parameter vanishes minimizers concentrate into sharp interfaces and the bulk energy collapses onto an effective surface energy penalizing the measure of those transition boundaries
brief_reason: The abstract defines E_epsilon as an integral of a well-potential term W(x, grad u)/epsilon plus epsilon times a second-gradient penalty and proves it Gamma-converges to a local interfacial energy on laminate-type configurations, which is the Modica-Mortola singular-perturbation sharp-interface limit.
